wider.d: recognised as the most active arranger in Taiwan's domestic syndicated market.
CTBC led the ~US$1.765bn debut (Feb 2026) — an AI-server name choosing CTBC.
MLAB with Bank SinoPac — but Taipower's freeze on new 5MW+ power approvals north of Taoyuan caps appetite.

gloss: Mandated Lead Arranger & Bookrunner — top role: structures the deal and runs the book. The fee-rich seat CTBC competes for.
Lower participation tiers; your title and fee scale with how much you commit.
Runs the syndication / administers the loan after signing.
Guarantees the amount even if other banks don't show up — more risk, more fee.
A slice of a loan with its own terms or currency.
Term loan = borrowed once, repaid on schedule. Revolver (RCF) = draw-repay-redraw, like a corporate credit card.
Repaid all at the end vs paid down over the life.
Length of the loan / average time the money is actually outstanding.
Options to upsize the loan if demand is strong.
Amend & Extend — push out maturities on an existing facility.
Interest above a benchmark, in basis points (100bp = 1.00%). “155bp over SOFR” = SOFR + 1.55%.
Margin + annualised fees = a lender's total yield.
One-time fee for joining, scaled by ticket size.
A minimum rate even if the benchmark falls (e.g. 2.5%).
SOFR (USD), Taibor (TWD), Hibor (HKD), BBSY (AUD), Tonar (JPY), LPR (China).
Covenant-lite — a loan with few maintenance covenants; common in LBOs, more lender risk.
One large loan funded by a group of banks.
Small deal among a few relationship banks, no broad syndication.
Leveraged buyout / Term Loan B — debt backing a PE acquisition; higher leverage and pricing.
Pricing tied to ESG / social targets; “social loan” funds a social purpose (e.g. education).
More demand than sought, so the deal is increased.
Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly & Test — ASE's business; packaging + testing chips.
Advanced packaging that integrates a GPU with its HBM stacks — the AI bottleneck.
High-Bandwidth Memory — the AI-GPU memory crowding out conventional DRAM and spiking prices.
